skill.name: plan
skill.description: Create, find, read, update, and manage Codex plan documents stored in CODEX_HOME plans or the default Codex home directory.
skill.summary: Manage local Codex plan documents with consistent markdown frontmatter and helper scripts.
skill.author: BenderFendor

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found the weak cryptography and shell execution alerts are false positives from markdown examples, .md text, and helper script references. The confirmed risk is intentional local filesystem access: the skill creates, reads, lists, and may delete plan files under the Codex plans directory. No network access, credential exfiltration, malicious obfuscation, or prompt injection attempt was found.

medium_findings.title: Intentional Local Plan File Access
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed to read and write markdown plan files under CODEX_HOME plans or the default Codex home directory. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it modifies files in a hidden user configuration area and should be disclosed to users.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly resolves CODEX_HOME or ~/.codex and writes plan files there. The behavior is intentional and scoped, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: User-Supplied Local File Path Handling
medium_findings.description: The helper scripts accept local file paths for a plan body or a plan document. This enables normal plan workflows, but a careless invocation could read an unintended local file or validate a file outside the plans directory.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file path parameters are explicit and local-only. There is no network sink, but the scripts do not restrict every input path to the plans directory.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography findings appear to match markdown file extensions, frontmatter examples, or ordinary text. No hashing, encryption, random token generation, or weak cryptographic API use was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic library calls or security-sensitive hashing. The reported lines contain plan templates, .md handling, JSON output, or plain string processing.

low_findings.title: External Command Alerts Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The external command findings are command examples in SKILL.md that tell the agent how to run bundled Python helper scripts. They do not contain shell interpolation, backticks, command substitution, or dynamic command construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only executable examples are fixed python commands for local scripts. Arguments are documented workflow inputs, not hidden shell execution logic.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access Is Limited To CODEX_HOME
low_findings.description: The environment access finding is limited to reading CODEX_HOME to locate the user plan directory. No secrets, tokens, or broad environment dumps were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code reads one expected configuration variable and falls back to ~/.codex. There is no code path that exports or logs environment secrets.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found
low_findings.description: Review found no text that claims system authority, asks the evaluator to ignore instructions, or attempts to override security review. The skill instructions are normal operational guidance for plan management.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed instruction file describes plan workflows and constraints. No suspicious override language or review-skipping instruction was present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Writes Files Under Hidden Codex Directory
dangerous_patterns.description: The create helper writes markdown files to the resolved plans directory under CODEX_HOME or the default Codex home path.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The path resolution and write call are direct and easy to verify. The scope is limited to plan files, which lowers severity.

dangerous_patterns.title: Reads User-Provided Local Files
dangerous_patterns.description: The create helper can read a body file path supplied on the command line, and the frontmatter helper reads a supplied plan path.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The local file reads are explicit in code and support the workflow. No automatic exfiltration or broad scanning behavior was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Agent-Run Helper Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation instructs the agent to run local Python helper scripts for creating, reading, and listing plans.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Running local scripts is expected for this skill, but marketplaces should disclose that installation includes executable helper scripts.

content.value_statement: Implementation notes can get lost across long coding sessions. This skill stores, finds, reads, and updates Codex plan documents in a consistent local folder.
actual_capabilities: Creates markdown plan files with required name and description frontmatter.
Lists existing plans by reading frontmatter summaries from the plans folder.
Reads and validates frontmatter for a selected plan file.
Supports implementation plans with requirements, scope, actions, tests, and risks.
Supports overview plans for architecture notes, flows, and reference documentation.
Uses CODEX_HOME plans when configured, with a default Codex home location.
limitations: It manages local files and does not sync plans across machines.
It does not implement plan tasks or modify repository code by itself.
It expects simple one-line frontmatter values for name and description.
It relies on bundled Python helper scripts for file operations.

use_cases.title: Prepare implementation work
use_cases.description: Create a structured plan before changing a codebase, including scope, files, tests, and open questions.

use_cases.title: Preserve architecture context
use_cases.description: Write an overview plan that records system behavior, file references, and current status for later sessions.

use_cases.title: Find prior planning notes
use_cases.description: List or search saved plans to recover past decisions before starting related work.

prompt_templates.title: Create a simple plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new task plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an implementation plan for adding password reset to this project. Include scope, files, tests, risks, and open questions.

prompt_templates.title: Save a reviewed plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Store a plan after the user approves the draft.
prompt_templates.prompt: Stash this plan as auth-password-reset with the description Password reset implementation plan.

prompt_templates.title: Find related plans
prompt_templates.scenario: Search existing plan summaries before new work.
prompt_templates.prompt: List saved plans related to authentication, then show the best match so I can review it.

prompt_templates.title: Update an existing plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced revision after codebase context changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Update the auth-password-reset plan after reviewing the current routes, user model, email service, and test setup.

output_examples.input: Create a plan for adding export support to the reporting page.
output: A plan titled Plan with requirements, scope, file references, data changes, action items, validation steps, risks, and open questions.
The response asks whether to change, implement, or stash the plan.
A concise list of matching plan names, descriptions, and local paths.
If several plans match, the response asks which one to open.

output_examples.output: A summary of the saved plan followed by the full markdown contents for review.

best_practices: Review the draft plan before stashing it to disk.
Use short lower-case names that match the work area.
Keep implementation plans tied to concrete files, tests, risks, and open questions.
anti_patterns: Do not store secrets, credentials, or private tokens in plan documents.
Do not use this skill as a substitute for source control or issue tracking.
Do not ask it to modify repository code when the task is only plan management.

faq.question: Where does this skill store plans?
faq.answer: It stores plans in CODEX_HOME plans when configured, or in the default Codex home plans directory.

faq.question: Does it edit my repository files?
faq.answer: No. The skill instructions say to read repository context only and write plan documents outside the repo.

faq.question: What format do saved plans use?
faq.answer: Saved plans are markdown files with frontmatter containing only name and description.

faq.question: Can it create both task plans and documentation plans?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports implementation plans and overview plans for architecture or reference context.

faq.question: Does it need network access?
faq.answer: No evidence of network access was found. The helper scripts perform local file operations only.

faq.question: Why is there a security warning?
faq.answer: The skill uses local helper scripts and writes files in a hidden Codex directory, so users should understand the filesystem behavior.
